trivia_bullets.text: Beastie Beatdown is a {R}{G} uncommon sorcery fitting the set's red-green 'Delirium Beatdown' draft archetype. Per the Wizards design article for DSK, delirium was added during Set Design (not Vision Design) because the set's enchantment-heavy card distribution and manifest dread mechanic naturally filled graveyards with multiple card types, making delirium achievable.
sources: magic.wizards.com: Top of the Duskmourning Part 2

trivia_bullets.text: The mechanic is a fight variant with a conditional upside: if delirium is active (four or more card types in graveyard), the controlled creature gets two +1/+1 counters before dealing damage. This makes Beastie Beatdown a power-scaling pseudo-fight -- it functions as removal when ahead on board, and the delirium bonus can turn a smaller creature into a credible threat before the fight resolves.
Illustrated by Inkognit (alias of Joao Fiuza), a creature and character design specialist. Inkognit has illustrated 42 or more Magic cards across multiple sets, per Gatherer's artist search.

EDHREC rank 14,083 and penny_rank 12,503 confirm limited Commander and Penny Dreadful adoption. The card is narrow -- it requires a creature you control and a creature an opponent controls as mandatory targets, meaning it has no utility in empty-board situations. Its home is Limited, where delirium red-green is a supported draft archetype in DSK.

related_cards.name: Ram Through
related_cards.note: Closest mechanical predecessor -- green fight-with-trample sorcery

related_cards.name: Ulvenwald Tracker
related_cards.note: Classic green fight enabler; design ancestry for green fight spells in graveyard-heavy formats
